title: haplopappus spinulosus subsp. gooddingii
intro: Haplopappus spinulosus subsp. gooddingii, also known as Goodding's rabbitbrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Haplopappus spinulosus subsp. gooddingii can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
